technical_terms_dropped: bhikṣu (the renouncing-mendicant — the specific category is not named, only the act)
verdict_reason: The engineering layer does not invoke a sectarian-specific reading of bhaikṣya or rudhira-pradigdha. It uses the consensus reading (Arjuna's argument is a false-equivalence — the projected post-action life is unfairly worse than the idealised pre-action escape). Both Shankara and Ramanuja agree the verse describes Arjuna's distorted projection without endorsing it; the engineering layer preserves this. The bhikṣu-as-renunciate technical term is not named, but the verse explicitly does not yet engage the karma-sannyāsa doctrine (chapter 5); naming bhikṣu here would pre-empt later teaching. Acceptable. PASS.
